terms.definition: For a discrete random variable, the function giving P(X = x) at each possible value x; masses are non-negative and sum to 1.
For a continuous random variable, a non-negative function f whose integral over an interval gives the probability that X falls in that interval.
Synonym for the Normal distribution N(μ, σ²), a continuous bell-shaped density parameterized by mean μ and variance σ².
A discrete distribution over K classes with probabilities π_1,…,π_K summing to 1; one trial of the Multinomial family.
The average expected count in the Poisson model; both mean and variance of X equal λ.
Maps a vector of logits to class probabilities π_k = exp(z_k) / Σ_j exp(z_j), parameterizing a Categorical distribution at the output layer.
